transcript: [pitch-shifted] Hi! I’m doin’ good! How’s everybody doin’?
[MC laughs, crowd laughs, cheers, claps, and whistles]
Yeah, we’re right here in London and, uh… we just watched the movie. Thank you for phoning. Does anyb—?
Thank you! I’m happy to be with ya tonight!
Does anybody have any questions, uh… for Mr. LPC? Or want to—?
Oh! Sure! Oh, yeah! [exhales] I’m happy to talk to ya.—
Okay! Yeah. We got a question.
[in background to MC] …about the guy who’s, like, the… Global Elite? Did he know who the kid was before he called?
So they’re asking— the “Global Elite” call, uh…
[[Game Stop 2|Game Stop [2] ]]. Did you… have inside information on that guy, or did you know him, or, uh…?—
[delay] Yeah!No, I didn’t know him. I didn’t know him.—

Somebody suggested that he would be… funny to call. I thought it was just sorta there. I just thought it was sorta “Okay! Good enough for a bonus feature.” But people have told me that they really like it and, uh… were really into it, so… okay.—
The number you have reached it not available from this service. oh-six-zero-[redacted]
Um… ye— There’s a few calls: one that stands out is “Moisturization”. Uh… George’s Bar. What’s the story with that guy?
Yeah, what’s the story with George?

Yeah! Uh… used to have sort of unorthodox methods for getting phone numbers to call. So, say I had, uh… Dave’s number, here, and I’d go to call him but I’d change the last two numbers and see who I got. And that was how I got George’s Bar—his cellphone number. And…
He liked to talk and talk and act tough and get all tough and, uh… so I called him a lot and, uh… he had a very unfortunate shootout at his establishment ‘couple years ago. Yeah! [exhales]
Hey, um… is this the butcher shop up ‘ere?
Yeah, this’s the butcher shop, eh.
Hey man, I’m looking to get some… Maniac Loaf, if you can put some together for me tonight.
Uh… ya know, it has leather and sticks and marshmallows—an old country favorite! [exhales]
So… is that a custom order? How much would that be on a custom basis? [exhales]
Used to be a cop, he used to be a D.E.A agent. He used to be a lotta different things, he said.
I think he lost a few family members in that shootout, didn’t he?
Yeah! Ah man, it was… super bad.
I have a question: do you—?
To me, there’s almo— I don’t know if it’s an actual “LPC Curse” but a lot of people have died that you’ve phoned.
I was thinking about that and somebody…
Hey listen, I need to get some, uh… some leg bone.
Yeah, how much is that gon’ run me, guy?
It’s $4.99 a kilo for leg bones.

[sample of Wayne Bent disciple]My mom told me one time that she thought I was “brainwashed”. And I thought, Yeah! I am brainwashed! Michael has washed my brain of all my own corrupt thoughts! [laughs][pitch-shifted] [sample of Wayne Bent cultists cheering, shouting “Liberty!”]
Okay, can I get some skin from you guys up ‘ere? No?Okay, uh, he left. [inhales] But anyways, so yeah…—
That’s an Australian butcher. Sure, sure, sure!—
Sure, indeed! We’re gonna call the guy from Pawn Stars, here… in Las Vegas. Ooh! You betcha!
[laughs] Not Chumlee, the, uh… the owner Rick.
And it’s… dialing. And, uh… yeah, let’s see what’s goin’ on up there!
Uh… Hey! How ya doin’, man?

Yeah, I got, a… big poster of Evonne Goolagong kind of in action—swingin’ the tennis racket. ’S lookin’ for a 30-day loan on it. Evonne Goolagong? [exhales] Just wanted ta— I’m just lookin’ to stay off the books, though. [exhales]
Well he’s out! It was… worth a try!—
Thank you, thank you, thank you! Thank you for bein’ here. That happens, here in LPC World. That’s gonna be fine. We’re gonna roll right along. Not an issue!
We’ve got a number: 4-3-9—[redacted]. This is Jessie; he’s a musician in Montreal.
”Cox”, I said. How are you doin’, man?
Wait, w— Brock, one second, one second. Brock? What’s your last name?
Yeah but why—? Why are you calling me?
Well I’m playing drums here and I wanted you to, uh… get a feel for my style. You know what I’m— what I’m sayin’?
That funny to you, is it?
Nah man, I’m feelin’ the rhythm, dude. You remind me of, like. Tony Allen or like— like a young Max Roach, man, like that.
Elvin Jones? Elv—? Oh, big time, big time!
Yeah, man! Elvin— You know it, dude! I can feel the polyrhythms.
Well yeah, that’s the problem, is my ankles are all jacked, man! You… consider just rubbin’ my ankles down with some ice packs or somethin’? Than we call really rock out!—
Man, I’d love to rub your ankles! Nah, real— real talk, man. What’re you callin’ me from L.A.? Who is this?
No, I got a new phone, that’s all!
Who is this? Who—? Wha—? Who are you, Brock?
Yeah but how do you know me?
What did you think of it?
It’s okay, man! It’s not that— I’m— I’m trying to figure out—you know—like, stay away from that these days, ya know? Like, just, like, just doin’ it, like, on the low, you know what I’m sayin’?
Yeah, I do. You know where to get any Goofy Loaf or anything like that?

Uh… No, I don’t know— I don’t know, um… I don’t really know where to get that, but… if you kinda wanted some Latin inspiration, like, you probably listen to, like, Sabu Martinez; that’s some great percussion!—
Well we got another one, here.[to audience member]Oh, do you have a question?

Yeah, I was kinda curious. You’ve— Like, you’ve had a lot of people that you called repeatedly and repeatedly. Is what you’re doing kind of like a— like a form of intimacy for you or is, like—? Do you find you get too close to people sometimes and have to back off?
The question is: the people you call repeatedly—do you ever feel intimate with them and do you have to back off?

Yeah, I had an experience once when I was a teenager. They used to have a thing: person-to-person calls. If you called collect to a specific person then they would either accept or decline it. And I ended up talking to this woman operator for… a half an hour. And she ended up calling me back at home and talked a lot on the phone. We were supposed to meet one time but we never did that. But oh, yeah! You know, you get into these situations, yeah.
Oh good, uh… what’s— What is this place?
Oh yeah, yeah, uh… d’you have cassettes up ‘ere?
Oh, yeah. Yeah, do you have anything by, uh… Fiddlebone on cassette at all?
It’s my sister’s favorite. Lemme just have you talk to her, okay? ‘Cause she knows more about it than I do.
[sample of Wayne Bent disciple]I’ve been having this thought that I just needed to come up here and take off… all my clothes! Really! So I said, “Well… Shall I?”
Anyway, yeah, she’s not ready. I guess she doesn’t know the, uh…
…the particular album title. Oh wait, here, yeah!

[samples of Wayne Bent disciples]I think I really feel relief that it’s the end.Yeah!It’s like that’s what Father’s saying now: just a little bit longer and… you’re gonna come home.Mhm!Where you were meant to be!''Yeah!It’s like, we weren’t made to live on this Earth away from Father.
Okay. She’s gone. She’s gone, alright!
Not an issue! It’s… par for the course in LPC Land. Just keep on truckin’.
Hey, uh, I need to get some skin, alright? And some blood—couple, uh… ounces o’ blood for a special recipe. [sniffs]
Okay. Um, we don’t have any blood but we do have, um, silverskin [?], which is, um, the intestine.
Okay, um… Hey, can I get any regurgitation at all? It’s—it’s supposed to be, uh…—
No. It’s unavailable. Sent straight to the, um… fat and bone room and then gets turned into something else.
Huh! Could you, uh, divert some for me, here, on my cookout? I got a big cookout tonight!
Oh no, couldn’t do any. We don’t do any [unclear] around here, mate.—
Okay, uh… you guys got any semen at all up ‘ere? [exhales]
No, I don’t have any mate, no.
Where—? Where would I go for sumpin’ like ‘at? [exhales]
Not too sure, mate! Nah, I’ve never seen it available!
[ Wayne Bent samples ]But I would have more authority to touch the Soul than an MD would. MDs aren’t prepared for what I’ve had to do.—Uh… MDs are with naked people. MDs even put their fingers—
Okay so, uh… what—? D’you have hoofs at all? In stock?
Hoofs? Okay. And, uh, what about the regurgitation? How m—? How much is that, or where—? Where—? At what point can I get that, uh, first of the month, or…?
Uh… we don’t sell it at all, mate.
You can make an exception, can’t ye?
No, we don’t h— We don’t do slaughter in here, mate. Exactly.
Uh-huh. Alright! Lemme just get some o’ your urine then and then, uh, we’ll be good to go, alright?
Yeah, too easy, mate! No worries! How many ounces you after?
Six ounces? No worries, mate. I think you’re takin’ the piss enough. You got enough of your own.
What is that supposed to mean?
We’ve got a number for you here, sir.

[ Wayne Bent sample ][delay]—Yes. Yes.—[sample of Wayne Bent disciple][delay] I know that people think we’re gonna commit suicide, but I can’t… comprehend it in my mind.[ Wayne Bent samples ][delay] It is a defining moment and God’s people are going to be vindicated and the world is going to be judged. It’s final. You can take that to the bank. We’re done. We’re talking about leaving the Earth—And nakedness is another symbol of our relationship with God.
Alright. I’ve got about… seven more minutes, here. So we’ve got a few more numbers for ye.
5-1-9—[redacted]. This is Edy. He’s a promoter.
Edy! What’s cookin’? Alex Trebek? Who—who is this? Hey!
[ Wayne Bent sample ][delay] I am Messiah.
[ Wayne Bent sample ][delay] I am Messiah.
Hey, I need to get some, uh, photographs taken. Can you… maybe help me out?
This is Grasidious Fike. I’m involved in cave exploration and, uh, sorcery—things like that. What’s, uh… what’s on your calendar? Got time for some photographs up here.
Sorry, I don’t know what the fuck you’re talking about and I don’t even know who this is, but, uh…
I need some frickin’ photos, guy! Why don’t you listen up? And then we can put some ideas together. Whadda you think?
You want me to come over and shove you around a little bit? [exhales]
Yeah, you… gonna say the same thing when you feel my frickin’ grip?! [exhales]
Aw, is that funny to ya, huh? I’m gonna touch your, uh, leg bone here pretty soon. Give ya an Indian burn; whadda ya think of that? [exhales]
[ Wayne Bent sample ][delay] Do what God tells you!
Oh! Wow! That guy was ready for some, uh… action, I think. But… only half ready—
Well we’ve got three minutes left.
Emilio Se— Yes. Lemme go find him.

Uh, this is… Guy. Alright? And, uh… listen, I need to… borrow you guys’ living room. I got your number online. We’re just lookin’ to have a card game—a friendly card game! Just need to borrow your living room for a couple hours. Uh… this week sometime.—
I don’t even know who you are.
I’m in Shaw Heights. It’s fine. Not a problem. Not a problem there.
No, but… I don’t do stuff like that.
I don’t even know who “Guy” is.
Huh? I don’t even know you!—
Well we’re gonna get together and play cards, aren’t we? You’ll get to know me then.—
I don’t even know you. Why would I “get together” with you?
Well I got cigars, I got poker chips, I got a deck o’ cards, I got about three or four good ol’ boys with me; it’ll be a great time! It’ll be a great time!—
I don’t even know you. Quit calling this number.—
We’re gonna bor— We’re gonna borrow your living room this week, okay? For about two hours.
What’s a good time to come up ‘ere? Can you just gimme a good time?
What’s wrong with you? Are you, uh… have, uh… mental issues or what?

D’you guys have any ground beef up there? ‘Cause we need to cook some stew and stuff when we get there, okay? I’ll be there tomorrow—we can just work it out—between seven and nine o’clock at night. Okay?
No, you’re— I think you need, uh, take some , uh, mental medication!
What’s that supposed to mean? This is gonna be a Wednesday night card game!—
That means you’re a crazy guy!
It’s gonna be a—a Thursday night card game! That’s all there is to it! Okay?
No, there’s nothin’ to it. You’re just a weirdo.
Hey, I got your number online!—
Wher—? Where’d you get it? Whadda you mean “online”? From where?
Aren’t you in The Community? Are you in The Community or aren’t ya?
Look, just go pick up some ground beef and set up your card table and then we’ll be there at seven o’clock, okay? Seven o’clock sharp! You got me?—
No! Quit calling— Quit calling this number!—
You got—? I will because we’re just gonna come over, alright? And then have your— have your c—
What is that supposed to mean?—
Lemme c— Lemme, uh— Lemme report you to the police officer for… uh, for harassment.
[samples of Wayne Bent disciples]It’s sworn in the bible—it will occur!Mhm!—
What’s your name again? Guy what? Wha—?
And what’s your address? Lemme have your address.—
I’m in Shaw Heights. Hey, buddy! I got your number online! You know, you si—
It doesn’t matter where you got my number. What’s your address?—
You signed up for this! Don’t you remember?—
No, I didn’t sign up for anything!
I’m gonna… show up at your address.—
So we can go over there with a police officer and get this, uh, squared away!
[ Wayne Bent sample ]I am the embodiment of God. I am… Divinity and humanity combined.
Alright? So that’s all you need to know.—
No, sir, you’re a psycho! You need to take some medication.
I got a six pack o’ beer!—
I’m gonna show up with a beer and, uh, some cards and, uh, cigars.—
Well I’m gonna show up with a fuckin’ gun and blow your fuckin’ brains out if you keep fucking with me! So quit fucking calling over here!—
We’re just gonna play a friendly game o’ cards! We’re— You’re gonna love it. You’re gonna love it when—
Alright, if you wanna— If you wanna— If you wanna fuckin’ die…
…come fuck with me, then. You piece of shit.—
I’m br— I’m gonna br— I’m gonna bring—
Dino, I’m gonna bring, uh, Ry-Ry. I’m gonna bring ‘em all over with me!
Okay? Canada, you’ve been astounding! You’ve been astounding!
[delay] We love you! I love you! Thank you! Thank you so much! We love you, I love you! Thanks for having a great time tonight. Thank you! Woo! Have a great night! Thank you so much!
